title: Introducing Gemini 3.5 Live Translate
summary: Gemini 3.5 Live Translate is Google's speech-to-speech translation model supporting 70+ languages with real-time multilingual translation and automatic language switching. It enables live dubbing of video content and natural-sounding translations, available to developers via the Gemini API and integrated into Google Translate and Google Meet.
writeup: Google announces Gemini 3.5 Live Translate, a speech-to-speech translation model now open to developers through the Gemini API, after powering Google Translate internally. The video demonstrates two live applications: real-time audio dubbing of streaming tab content and a presenter-to-audience relay system where attendees join via URL or QR code and each hear the talk in their own preferred language. The model handles automatic language switch detection mid-stream with no manual configuration, producing output described as flowing and natural without choppiness.

key_points: Gemini 3.5 Live Translate is available today on the Gemini API and in AI Studio, supporting real-time input and output across 70 plus languages.
The live dubbing demo streams translated audio directly from a browser tab, demonstrated by re-voicing a Google I/O keynote segment into Hindi.
The live presentation demo lets a speaker create a session that attendees join via a URL or QR code, with each listener independently selecting their preferred output language.
The model detects mid-stream language switches automatically: when the presenter switched to Sinhala, the output shifted to English without any configuration change.
The capability is also rolling out in private preview on Google Meet, and is accessible to consumers through Google Translate on iOS and Android with any pair of headphones.
